app.name: n8n
app.bestFor: The top pick when you want AI baked in, not bolted on — native agent nodes, RAG and tool-calling, self-hostable for full data control.
app.rankBasis: Consistently appears in top positions in AI-workflow roundups from n8n, TechnologyAdvice, and Digital Applied; its GitHub repository has about 197.8k stars and community discussions repeatedly position it as the technical, self-hostable alternative.
app.pricingFree: Cloud Free: 50 executions/month, 1 user, and 1 shared project; Community Edition is also self-hosted
app.pricingPaid: Starter €20/month billed annually (€24 monthly) — 2,500 workflow executions/month, 5 concurrent executions, and 2,300 AI credits/month
features: visual workflow canvas with JavaScript and Python
AI agents, tool use, and RAG workflows
self-hosting with RBAC and audit trails
pros: high flexibility for technical teams and unusual workflows
self-hosting gives stronger data and deployment control
execution-based billing can be more predictable than per-step billing
cons: steeper learning curve than simpler no-code tools
self-hosting adds upgrades, backups, and operational work
community support is stronger than dedicated support on lower tiers

app.name: Zapier
app.bestFor: Fast, event-driven SaaS automations for non-technical teams, with an 8,000+ app connector catalog and minimal setup.
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly included in broad workflow-automation roundups from TechnologyAdvice, Domo, and Intuz, with a large G2/Capterra review footprint and the strongest mainstream adoption signal among no-code automation tools.
app.pricingFree: Free forever — 100 tasks/month with Zaps, Tables, and Forms
app.pricingPaid: Professional from $19.99/month — task-based plans starting at 750 tasks/month
pros: fastest setup for common business automations
broad connector catalog and mature templates
accessible to non-technical teams
cons: task-based pricing becomes expensive at higher volume
complex branching and data transformations are less flexible
reviewers often report limits around debugging and support

app.name: Make
app.bestFor: High-volume, multi-branch workflows where visual debugging and deterministic routing matter most.
app.rankBasis: Repeatedly shortlisted alongside Zapier and n8n in TechnologyAdvice, Digital Applied, and Intuz comparisons, with a substantial G2/Capterra review base and strong community mindshare for visual scenario building.
pros: visual routing and branching are more expressive than basic Zaps
good feature-to-price ratio for multi-step scenarios
strong execution visibility for debugging
cons: the visual builder has a noticeable learning curve
large scenarios can become difficult to read and maintain
reviewers report occasional support and documentation gaps

app.name: Gumloop
app.bestFor: A no-code AI-first builder bundling visual flows, model connectors, hosted MCP and a build-assistant agent ("Gummie") in one place.
app.rankBasis: Appears in the n8n AI-workflow roundup and Vellum's low-code AI list, but has a smaller independent review footprint than the established leaders; the ranking is driven mainly by its AI-first workflow positioning.
app.pricingPaid: Pro starts at $37/month — 20k+ credits/month, with a 14-day free trial
pros: fast way to prototype AI agents and automations
AI models and business integrations sit in one visual builder
strong fit for teams experimenting with agent workflows
cons: smaller independent review and community footprint
credit-based usage can be harder to forecast
less mature general-purpose automation coverage than Zapier or n8n

app.name: Microsoft Power Automate
app.bestFor: Enterprise workflow and RPA automation deeply wired into Microsoft 365 and the Power Platform.
app.rankBasis: Included in TechnologyAdvice and VisioneerIT enterprise automation roundups and backed by a large G2/Capterra review base; Microsoft 365 reach, governance, and RPA are its clearest adoption signals.
app.pricingPaid: Power Automate Premium $15/user/month paid yearly — cloud flows, attended desktop flows, process mining, and 250 MB Dataverse database plus 2 GB file
pros: deep integration with Microsoft 365, Dataverse, and Azure
broad enterprise governance and connector coverage
combines cloud workflow automation with desktop RPA
cons: licensing and connector entitlements are difficult to understand
the platform has a substantial learning curve
users report brittle flows and troubleshooting friction in some RPA scenarios

app.name: Activepieces
app.bestFor: Open-source Zapier alternative where each of ~750 integrations is also exposed as an MCP server for agents.
app.rankBasis: Appears in Zapier-alternative roundups from Composio and Agensi, and its public repository has about 23.4k GitHub stars; community coverage is smaller than n8n's but unusually strong for an open-source alternative.
app.pricingFree: Cloud Free — 10 active flows and unlimited runs; self-hosting is free
app.pricingPaid: Cloud paid usage — $5 per active flow/month after the 10 free active flows
pros: open-source and self-hostable with a low-cost cloud option
intuitive interface for technical and non-technical users
TypeScript pieces make custom integrations approachable
cons: smaller connector and template ecosystem than Zapier or n8n
self-hosted deployments still require maintenance
rapid product changes can leave edge cases and integrations uneven

app.name: Workato
app.bestFor: Enterprise iPaaS for governed, cross-department integration and automation at scale.
app.rankBasis: Included in TechnologyAdvice's enterprise automation roundup and supported by a large G2/Capterra enterprise-review footprint; its iPaaS adoption, governance, and 1,200+ connector catalog are the clearest ranking signals.
app.pricingPaid: Consumption-based enterprise pricing (no public rate; contact sales)
pros: strong enterprise integration breadth and governance
good fit for cross-department automation at scale
enterprise support and implementation ecosystem
cons: sales-led pricing is difficult to compare publicly
implementation and administration are heavier than lightweight tools
cost is commonly cited as a barrier for smaller teams

skill.name: n8n
skill.bestFor: The dominant open-source workflow-automation platform (~197.8k★) — 400+ integrations, native AI/agent nodes, fully self-hostable. The de-facto leader.
skill.rankBasis: About 197.8k GitHub stars make n8n the runaway adoption leader in this track; its official repository describes 1,500+ integrations, AI-native workflows, and self-hosting, while its community is one of the most active in this category.
pros: largest integration and template ecosystem in this set
combines visual building with real code flexibility
strong community and self-hosting control
cons: self-hosting requires operational ownership
fair-code licensing is more restrictive than a permissive OSS license
the breadth of nodes and options can overwhelm new users

skill.name: Activepieces
skill.bestFor: The leading MIT-licensed Zapier alternative (~23.4k★) — self-hostable with a TypeScript pieces framework and AI-agent/MCP support.
skill.rankBasis: About 23.4k GitHub stars place Activepieces among the most-adopted open-source Zapier alternatives; its MIT license, active TypeScript pieces ecosystem, and community Discord provide stronger signals than most newer competitors.
pros: permissive MIT license and straightforward self-hosting
custom integrations fit naturally into a TypeScript codebase
friendly UI and relatively quick learning curve
cons: smaller ecosystem and less maturity than n8n
self-hosting still shifts upgrades and reliability work to the user
community-contributed pieces vary in depth and maintenance

skill.name: Node-RED
skill.bestFor: Mature low-code, event-driven flow tool (~23.4k★, IBM origin) — the standard for wiring together devices, APIs and services, huge in IoT.
skill.rankBasis: About 23.4k GitHub stars, more than 5,000 community nodes, and a long-running Node.js/IBM lineage make Node-RED one of the most established low-code event-driven tools in this category.
features: shareable JSON flows and reusable templates
pros: mature, lightweight, and especially strong for IoT and edge use
large community node and flow library
easy to run on inexpensive hardware or in the cloud
cons: large flows can become hard to organize and review
custom-node quality and maintenance vary across the ecosystem
less built-in governance and testing than enterprise orchestrators

skill.name: Windmill
skill.bestFor: Fast Rust-based developer platform (~17.3k★) that turns scripts into workflows, UIs and cron jobs — for engineers who want code over drag-and-drop.
skill.rankBasis: About 17.3k GitHub stars and active Discord/docs adoption support its position as a leading code-first alternative; the repository is explicitly positioned for APIs, background jobs, workflows, and internal UIs.
skill.pricingFree: Free Community Edition — open-source and self-hostable
features: scripts in Python, TypeScript, Go, Bash, SQL, and Rust
pros: excellent fit for engineers who want code instead of drag-and-drop
turns scripts into reusable workflows, APIs, and UIs
fast self-hosting with strong infrastructure control
cons: developer-centric model is not ideal for business-only users
self-hosting and worker sizing require engineering judgment
smaller connector ecosystem and review base than mainstream SaaS tools

skill.name: Trigger.dev
skill.bestFor: Code-first background jobs and AI-agent workflows (~15.7k★) in TypeScript — durable, long-running tasks for developers.
skill.rankBasis: About 15.7k GitHub stars, an active Discord, and a maintained Apache-2.0 repository support its position as a leading code-first workflow runtime for TypeScript AI tasks.
skill.pricingFree: Free & open-source (self-host); Trigger.dev Cloud Free includes $5/month in credits
pros: durable execution is a strong fit for long AI jobs
excellent TypeScript developer experience and observability
supports retries, queues, streaming, and human approval flows
cons: code-first design excludes non-technical workflow builders
production deployments still require careful concurrency and cost planning
smaller general-purpose integration ecosystem than n8n or Zapier

skill.name: n8n-skills (Członkowski)
skill.bestFor: The de-facto Claude Code skill pack for workflow automation (~5.9k★) — 14 skills for building production n8n workflows, by the author of n8n-mcp.
skill.rankBasis: About 5.9k GitHub stars make this the most-adopted dedicated n8n skill pack in the list; its README documents 14 complementary skills, hooks, and patterns based on more than 2,600 n8n templates.
pros: unusually comprehensive guidance for AI-assisted n8n building
validation-focused patterns reduce common configuration mistakes
large adoption signal for a workflow-specific skill pack
cons: requires n8n and the n8n-mcp server to be useful
unofficial guidance can drift as n8n changes
a skill pack is not itself a workflow runtime

skill.name: Kestra
skill.bestFor: Event-driven, YAML-declarative orchestration and scheduling platform (~27.4k★) — broader/data-oriented, bridging workflow automation and pipeline orchestration.
skill.rankBasis: About 27.5k GitHub stars and an active official Slack/community support a strong adoption signal, but Kestra is more data, AI, and infrastructure orchestration than lightweight SaaS automation, so it ranks below the direct workflow tools.
skill.pricingPaid: Kestra Cloud is consumption-based with no public flat rate; Enterprise pricing is contact-sales
features: hundreds of plugins for data, APIs, and infrastructure
retries, backfills, branching, and Git versioning
pros: strong production resilience and orchestration semantics
YAML plus UI and Git workflows suit infrastructure-minded teams
broad plugin ecosystem across data and cloud systems
cons: more infrastructure-oriented than simple business automations
YAML and orchestration concepts add complexity for beginners
self-hosting a production cluster is a substantial operational commitment

skill.name: n8n Skills (official, n8n-io)
skill.bestFor: The official n8n skill repo (~363★) — authoritative, first-party guidance for building n8n workflows in Claude Code; newer and smaller than Członkowski's pack.
skill.rankBasis: The official n8n skills repository has about 363 GitHub stars and first-party authority; its 13 capability skills, 50+ reference documents, and hook-based workflow guidance make it more authoritative but much smaller than the leading community pack.
features: guidance for agents, loops, errors, and credentials
pros: first-party guidance tracks n8n's supported MCP workflow
hooks nudge agents toward the right skill before risky actions
focused coverage of the full workflow lifecycle
cons: requires an n8n instance with instance-level MCP enabled
newer and much smaller community footprint
not a standalone automation engine or connector runtime

skill.name: n8n-skills (haunchen)
skill.bestFor: Independent n8n skill suite (~377★) documenting nodes and curated templates for Claude Code, Desktop and Web.
skill.rankBasis: About 378 GitHub stars and a maintained README covering 545 n8n nodes and 20 curated templates show meaningful specialist adoption, but the independent community signal remains small.
features: Claude Code, Claude Web, and Claude Desktop support
pros: broad node reference coverage for AI assistants
supports multiple Claude surfaces rather than one editor
includes compatibility checks and reusable templates
cons: small community and adoption base compared with the other n8n pack
pinned n8n-version support can create maintenance lag
documentation assists n8n but does not execute workflows itself

notes: Skill/Repo track rebuilt to rank by real adoption, not form factor. The recognized OSS workflow-automation platforms lead as kind:repo — n8n (~197.8k★) is the runaway leader, followed by Activepieces, Node-RED, Windmill and Trigger.dev — with the genuine Claude skills ranked among them: Członkowski's n8n-skills (~5.9k★) is the de-facto skill pack, plus the official n8n-io/skills and haunchen alternatives. The dedicated skill ecosystem here is almost entirely n8n-focused; Kestra is included as a broader (data-adjacent) orchestration option. Removed all MCP entries (Zapier MCP, n8n MCP, Activepieces MCP, Composio; note czlonkowski/n8n-mcp ~22k★ is an MCP server, excluded). Huginn (~49.7k★) and Automatisch were left out as stalled/slow-maintenance. Star counts verified live 2026-07-24.
